After your attack, you may switch Rocket's Scyther ex with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Rocket's Scyther ex can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 10 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Torchic ☆.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon.

rules_text.en: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card. Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.

text.en: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.
Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.
You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Piloswine does 30 damage to itself.

Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon's attacks. Rainbow Moves copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Togetic's type is still Colorless. Togetic performs that attack.
Does 30 damage to each Defending Pokémon.
Does 10 damage times the number of Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost)."
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.
The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. Put 5 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Tyranitar.
Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 70 damage plus 50 more damage.
Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Dark Ampharos.
If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, put 4 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If 2 of them are heads, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are heads, put 10 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are tails, remove all damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.
Choose up to 3 cards in your hand and put them on top of your deck. Then, search your deck for that many cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Before doing damage, you may flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 damage instead. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy card attached to Quagsire but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Search your deck for up to 4 different types of basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Dark Arbok. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Dark Arbok's, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Remove from Dark Crobat a number of damage counters equal to the number of your opponent's Pokémon in play.
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Each Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each Colorless Energy in that Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Dark Flaaffy can't use Thunder Slash during your next turn.
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Dark Golbat can't attack during your next turn. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to each of your Active Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.
You may discard an Energy card attached to Dark Golduck. If you do, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If you have less Benched Pokémon than your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each basic Energy card attached to all of your Active Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent has at least 2 Pokémon in play, move a basic Energy card from the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
You may move all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) Then, search your deck for a card that evolves from Dark Pupitar and put it on Dark Pupitar. (This counts as evolving Dark Pupitar.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Prize card more than your opponent.
Discard up to 2 Energy cards from your hand. Then, draw 2 cards for each Energy card you discarded.
Mantine can't attack during your next turn.

Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card or Rocket's Secret Machine card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, you may switch Rocket's Meowth with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin for each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's). This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads. Rocket's Meowth does 10 damage times the number of tails to itself.

Search your discard pile for Pokémon Tool cards and Rocket's Secret Machine cards. You may either show 1 Pokémon Tool card or Rocket's Secret Machine card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show a combination of 3 Pokémon Tool cards or Rocket's Secret Machine cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

rules_text.en: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card. Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, count the number of cards in your opponent's hand and draw that many cards.

Search your discard pile for Basic Pokémon and Evolution cards. You may either show 1 Basic Pokémon or Evolution card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show a combination of 3 Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

You may use this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent. Move 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon. Or, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card. Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player counts his or her Prize cards left and draws up to that many cards. (You draw your cards first.)
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. Each Pokémon with Dark or Rocket's in its name (both yours and your opponent's) gets +20 HP.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card. Discard a card from your hand. Then, draw 3 cards. If you discarded a Pokémon that has Dark or Rocket's in its name, draw 4 cards instead.
Search your deck for a Pokémon with Dark in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

text.en: You may discard a combination of up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards and Rocket's Secret Machine cards from your hand, and then do 20 damage plus 30 more damage for each card you discarded.

rules_text.en: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

Choose 1 of your Evolved Pokémon, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from it, and shuffle it into your deck (this counts as devolving that Pokémon). If that Pokémon remains in play, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon (this counts as evolving that Pokémon). Shuffle your deck afterward.

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) in play. (Any cards attached to that Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Place the first Basic Pokémon in the discard pile. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your Pokémon.

Attach Dark Metal Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Dark Metal Energy provides Darkness Energy and Metal Energy, but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play and has no effect other than providing Energy.)

R Energy can be attached only to a Pokémon that has Dark or Rocket's in its name. While in play, R Energy provides 2 Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.) If the Pokémon R Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). When your turn ends, discard R Energy.

text.en: Search your discard pile for a Water Energy card and attach it to Rocket's Articuno ex."
Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Rocket's Entei ex.
Discard 2 Energy attached to Rocket's Entei ex and then choose 1 of your opponent's Pok��mon. This attack does 60 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Discard an Energy card attached to Rocket's Mewtwo ex, and then switch all damage counters on Rocket's Mewtwo ex with those on the Defending Pokémon. (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
